title: Aureline connected-account registry seed: registry entry, provider descriptor, and publish-later object-seed vocabulary
description: Seed-level boundary schema for the connected-account / provider registry the early entry-path surfaces read. One registry entry binds a connected-provider record, a provider descriptor, the registry-side origin and target identity, the actor-scope class, the freshness window, and the publish-later object seeds the entry can mint. The schema is intentionally a thin seed that composes with — and never forks — the canonical record set in /schemas/providers/connected_account_record.schema.json, /schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json, and /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. It exists so the early provider-linked entry paths can render origin, target, actor scope, freshness, and publish-later object identity through one typed shape rather than re-inventing per-surface badges. Three entry kinds are exported: connected_account_registry_entry_record (one row in the registry), provider_descriptor_record (the descriptor a registry entry points at, separate from the diagnostic connected_provider_record), and publish_later_object_seed_record (an explicitly distinguishable publish-later or immediate-mutation seed the entry mints). Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary; entries carry opaque refs and structured fields only. Adding a new mutation_disposition_class, freshness_class, publish_later_object_class, registry_entry_status, or provider_capability_class is additive-minor and requires a connected_account_registry_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

connected_account_registry_schema_versio.description: Integer schema version for this connected-account-registry seed export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

entry_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show on desktop, CLI, notifications, provider-linked headers, docs/help, support exports, and audit rollups. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

provider_actor_class.description: Frozen provider-actor class vocabulary reused verbatim from /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden on every product, CLI, notification, provider-linked header, docs/help, support-export, queue-review, and audit surface; 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class.

provider_class.description: Frozen provider-class vocabulary reused verbatim from /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the registry entry was observed under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0007 and ADR-0010 redaction posture. Raw tokens, raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

mutation_disposition_class.description: Frozen disposition class the registry entry assigns to an attempted action. The disposition is rendered at the point of intent on every provider-linked entry path; publish_later_required and immediate_mutation_in_product MUST NOT collapse into one control. publish_later_required and browser_handoff_required entries MUST cite a publish_later_object_seed_record so the deferred object identity is explicit; inspect_only_no_mutation entries MUST NOT cite one.

freshness_class.description: Frozen freshness class the registry entry renders. never_observed means the registry has not yet observed the underlying connected provider; expired_beyond_window means the freshness horizon has elapsed and the entry MUST route to a re-observe path rather than claim a current state.

registry_entry_status.description: Frozen status class for the registry entry itself. seeded entries exist only to anchor future observation; live_observed entries have at least one healthy observation; degraded_observed entries name a non-healthy observation and route to repair; unobserved_pending entries are awaiting their first observation; revoked_or_disconnected entries route to reconnect or disconnect paths.

publish_later_object_class.description: Frozen seed-level publish-later object class. local_draft_only is reversible-by-default; queued_publish_pending and deferred_publish_in_queue are publish-later objects awaiting drain; browser_handoff_pending names an object whose mutation will be completed in the system browser; imported_read_only_snapshot is inspect-only. Surfaces MUST render exactly one class; inventing a per-surface class is forbidden.

provider_capability_class.description: Frozen descriptor capability class. A provider descriptor names the set of capabilities its registry entries may legitimately request. Entries MUST NOT request a disposition the descriptor does not declare; the request is refused at registry mint.

object_class.description: Provider-side object class the publish-later seed targets. Aligned with the connected_account_record and publish_later_record object_class vocabularies so seeds, sub-records, and queue rows project onto the same axis.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the registry entry was minted under. Mirrors the ADR-0009 origin shape; raw provider URLs and raw tokens never appear here.

canonical_host.description: Canonical host the registry entry targets. Self-hosted and mirrored deployments substitute the host without changing the local object vocabulary.

tenant_or_org_scope.description: Tenant / org / project scope the target is bound under. Empty string only when the provider class is genuinely tenantless.

target_disclosure.description: Canonical provider-side target the registry entry routes to. Raw URLs are forbidden; canonical_host plus tenant_or_org_scope are the only identifiers the registry surfaces.

additional_actor_classes.description: Additional actor classes the registry entry recognizes when more than one is live (for example, a human account plus a project-scoped grant on the same provider).

scope_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence naming what the actor is allowed to do under this scope on this entry. Surfaces MUST render this rather than a generic 'connected' label.

actor_scope.description: Actor scope the registry entry is acting under. primary_actor_class MUST NOT be unknown_actor_class on a live_observed entry; an unknown actor MUST be rendered as a repair-only entry instead.

last_observed_at.description: Most recent observation timestamp. Required when freshness_class is fresh, stale_within_window, or expired_beyond_window; omitted only when freshness_class is never_observed.

freshness_horizon_seconds.description: Window after which a fresh observation is downgraded to stale_within_window. 0 is allowed only when freshness_class is never_observed.

freshness_window.description: Freshness window the registry entry was observed under. Surfaces MUST render the freshness class verbatim; never_observed entries MUST NOT claim a healthy observation.

connected_provider_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the connected_provider_record exported by /schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. The registry entry composes with that record; it does not redefine it.

provider_descriptor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider_descriptor_record this entry points at. Required so the entry's mutation_disposition_class can be checked against the descriptor's declared capabilities.

browser_handoff_routing_summary_ref.description: Opaque ref to a provider-side browser-handoff packet (provider_entry_browser_handoff_packet_record from /schemas/providers/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json) when the disposition requires one. Empty string when mutation_disposition_class is immediate_mutation_in_product or inspect_only_no_mutation.

publish_later_object_seed_refs.description: Opaque refs to publish_later_object_seed_record entries this registry entry can mint. publish_later_required and browser_handoff_required entries MUST list at least one ref; inspect_only_no_mutation entries MUST list none.

allOf.description: publish_later_required and browser_handoff_required dispositions MUST list at least one publish_later_object_seed_ref.
inspect_only_no_mutation entries MUST NOT list publish_later_object_seed_refs.
live_observed entries MUST NOT claim primary_actor_class unknown_actor_class.
browser_handoff_required dispositions MUST cite a browser_handoff_routing_summary_ref.
never_observed entries MUST carry registry_entry_status unobserved_pending or seeded.

connected_account_registry_entry_record.description: One row in the connected-account / provider registry. Names the connected_provider_record the entry runs against, the provider_descriptor it points at, its origin and target, the actor-scope it is acting under, its freshness window, its declared mutation disposition, and the publish-later object seeds it can mint. The entry composes with the canonical sub-records exported by /schemas/providers/connected_account_record.schema.json and /schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json; it does not redefine them.

tenant_or_org_scope.description: Tenant / org / project scope the descriptor is bound under. Empty string only when the provider class is genuinely tenantless.

supported_actor_classes.description: Set of actor classes this descriptor admits. A registry entry whose actor_scope.primary_actor_class is not in this set is refused at mint.

supported_capabilities.description: Set of capabilities this descriptor declares. The registry entry's mutation_disposition_class MUST resolve against this set; for example, publish_later_required requires supports_publish_later.

default_mutation_disposition_class.description: Disposition the descriptor presents when an entry has not yet selected one. MUST resolve against supported_capabilities.

freshness_horizon_seconds.description: Window after which a fresh observation against this descriptor is downgraded to stale_within_window.

descriptor_owner_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence naming who owns the descriptor (user, org-managed, tenant-managed, policy-injector-managed). Used by docs/help and support exports so 'who owns this' never collapses into a generic 'Connected' state.

provider_descriptor_record.description: Descriptor for one provider the registry recognizes. Separate from the diagnostic connected_provider_record so that the seed can describe a provider without yet observing one (for example, in docs/help corpora or onboarding rows).

publish_later_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publish_later_queue_item_record in /schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json when one exists. Required when publish_later_object_class is queued_publish_pending or deferred_publish_in_queue; empty string otherwise.

allOf.description: local_draft_only seeds MUST carry mutation_disposition_class immediate_mutation_in_product or inspect_only_no_mutation; they MUST NOT be marked as browser_handoff_required.
imported_read_only_snapshot seeds MUST carry mutation_disposition_class inspect_only_no_mutation.
queued_publish_pending and deferred_publish_in_queue seeds MUST cite a publish_later_queue_item_ref.
browser_handoff_pending seeds MUST carry mutation_disposition_class browser_handoff_required.

publish_later_object_seed_record.description: Explicitly distinguishable publish-later or immediate-mutation seed minted by a registry entry. Surfaces MUST render the publish_later_object_class verbatim; a publish-later object MUST NOT collapse into an immediate-mutation control on any provider-linked surface.
